explicit_text: The 1971 San Fernando earthquake is unforgettable to me. It struck around 6 a.m., and fortunately, no one was up at my house, though I had four grandchildren staying over. The quake caused massive destruction—streets were torn up, houses flattened, and even my 150-foot brick wall came down. I had to get first aid at Olive View–UCLA Medical Center, which was overwhelmed and partially collapsed. The earthquake changed the Valley forever.
